input.content:
KIRKLAND & ELLIS LLP
BILLING NARRATIVE — CLIENT MATTER 4892-001
CLIENT: Westbrook Industries, Inc. v. Pacific Rim Supply Chain, LLC
PERIOD: March 1-15, 2024

DATE       TIMEKEEPER            HOURS  DESCRIPTION
03/01/24   M. Chen (Partner)      2.40  Reviewed and analyzed plaintiff's first set of interrogatories; drafted preliminary responses to interrogatories 1-15; conferred with associate regarding document production timeline.

03/01/24   A. Singh (Associate)    3.70  Researched California law on trade secret misappropriation under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act; drafted memorandum analyzing elements of misappropriation claim; reviewed Silvaco Data Systems v. Intel Corp., 184 Cal. App. 4th 210 (2010).

03/02/24   M. Chen (Partner)      1.50  Conferred with client regarding settlement demand of $3,500,000.00; discussed litigation strategy and potential counterclaim for breach of the Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement dated January 5, 2023.

03/03/24   A. Singh (Associate)    4.20  Drafted motion for protective order regarding confidential business information; researched good cause standard under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26(c); analyzed In re Roman Catholic Archbishop of Portland, 661 F.3d 417 (9th Cir. 2011).

03/04/24   P. Lawson (Paralegal)   5.80  Organized and indexed 2,347 documents produced by plaintiff; prepared privilege log for 89 withheld documents; coded documents in Relativity for attorney review.

03/05/24   M. Chen (Partner)      0.80  Attended telephonic status conference with Judge Martinez; obtained extension of discovery deadline to April 30, 2024; reported conference results to client.

03/06/24   A. Singh (Associate)    2.90  Prepared deposition outline for plaintiff's CEO David Park; identified key documents for examination; drafted deposition notices for three additional witnesses.

03/07/24   M. Chen (Partner)      1.20  Reviewed and revised protective order motion; conferred with opposing counsel James Butler of Quinn Emanuel regarding stipulated protective order; negotiated confidentiality designations.

03/08/24   A. Singh (Associate)    6.10  Attended and defended deposition of client's VP of Engineering, Dr. Rachel Okafor; prepared witness for examination; interposed objections to 14 questions on privilege grounds.

03/11/24   M. Chen (Partner)      3.50  Reviewed expert report of Dr. Thomas Brennan regarding damages calculation; analyzed methodology for lost profits computation; conferred with damages expert regarding rebuttal opinions.

03/12/24   P. Lawson (Paralegal)   4.30  Filed motion for protective order with the court; served copies on all parties; calendared response deadline for March 26, 2024; updated case docket tracking spreadsheet.

03/13/24   A. Singh (Associate)    3.40  Drafted opposition to plaintiff's motion to compel production of source code; researched proportionality requirements under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26(b)(1); analyzed Waymo LLC v. Uber Technologies, Inc., No. 17-cv-00939 (N.D. Cal. 2017).

03/14/24   M. Chen (Partner)      2.80  Conferred with co-counsel regarding mediation preparation; drafted mediation brief outlining liability defenses and damages analysis; reviewed mediator's pre-session questionnaire.

03/15/24   A. Singh (Associate)    1.60  Finalized privilege log; reviewed and revised responses to interrogatories; prepared materials for client review and signature.

SUMMARY
Total Partner Hours:      12.20
Total Associate Hours:    21.90
Total Paralegal Hours:    10.10
Total All Timekeepers:    44.20
Total Fees (blended):     $36,850.00
Total Costs Advanced:     $2,145.00 (filing fees, deposition transcripts, Relativity hosting)

concepts.folio_definition: California State Courts are the judicial branch of the California government responsible for interpreting and applying the law in civil and criminal cases within the state.

A California Family Rights Act (CFRA) claim is a legal action initiated by an employee against their employer for not complying with the CFRA, which provides eligible employees with up to 12 weeks of unpaid leave in a 12-month period for certain family or medical reasons, such as the birth or adoption of a child, the employee's own serious health condition, or to care for a seriously ill family member. The claim typically arises when an employer denies an employee's request for CFRA leave or interferes with an employee's right to take CFRA leave, or retaliates against an employee for exercising their right to CFRA leave. Employers can face liability for violating the CFRA, which can result in significant damages for the affected employee.
The California Court of Appeal - 3d Dist. is a specific division of the California Court of Appeal that hears appeals from trial courts in the third appellate district of California.
The California Court of Appeal - 5th Dist. is a specific division of the California Court of Appeal that hears appeals from trial courts in the central California region.
The California Court of Appeal - 1st Dist. is a specific division of the California Court of Appeal, which is a higher court that hears appeals from lower courts in California.

ruler_concepts.folio_definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.
A person or entity that is obtaining legal services from a legal service provider

A limited liability company (LLC) is a corporate structure in the United States whereby the owners are not personally liable for the company's debts or liabilities. Limited liability companies are hybrid entities that combine the characteristics of a corporation with those of a partnership or sole proprietorship.
A partner is an individual who is a member of a partnership - an entity formed when at least two or more individuals agree to go into business with one another.
A plaintiff, complainant, or claimant is a person who brings a civil or criminal action against another person or entity in a dispute.
Interrogatories are written questions posed by one party to another in a legal proceeding, typically used in the discovery phase of a lawsuit in order to obtain information or evidence.

Interrogatories Practice refers to the specific legal practice of using written questions to obtain information from the opposing party in a legal matter, falling under the broader category of Written Discovery Practice.
Responses to Interrogatories are written answers to a set of questions posed by one party to another during the discovery phase of a legal proceeding.

Requests for Production Practice refers to the legal process of requesting and obtaining documents or other tangible items from the opposing party in a legal matter, typically as part of the discovery process.

KIRKLAND & ELLIS LLP
BILLING NARRATIVE — CLIENT MATTER 4892-001
CLIENT:

Westbrook Industries, Inc. v. Pacific Rim Supply Chain, LLC
PERIOD:

March 1-15, 2024

DATE TIMEKEEPER HOURS DESCRIPTION
03/01/24 M. Chen (Partner) 2.40 Reviewed and analyzed plaintiff's first set of interrogatories; drafted preliminary responses to interrogatories 1-15; conferred with associate regarding document production timeline.

03/01/24 A. Singh (Associate) 3.70 Researched California law on trade secret misappropriation under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act; drafted memorandum analyzing elements of misappropriation claim; reviewed Silvaco Data Systems v. Intel Corp., 184 Cal.

03/02/24 M. Chen (Partner) 1.50 Conferred with client regarding settlement demand of $3,500,000.00; discussed litigation strategy and potential counterclaim for breach of the Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement dated January 5, 2023.

03/03/24 A. Singh (Associate) 4.20 Drafted motion for protective order regarding confidential business information; researched good cause standard under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26(c); analyzed In re Roman Catholic Archbishop of Portland, 661 F.3d 417 (9th Cir. 2011).
03/04/24 P. Lawson (Paralegal) 5.80 Organized and indexed 2,347 documents produced by plaintiff; prepared privilege log for 89 withheld documents; coded documents in Relativity for attorney review.
03/05/24 M. Chen (Partner) 0.80 Attended telephonic status conference with Judge Martinez; obtained extension of discovery deadline to April 30, 2024; reported conference results to client.
03/06/24 A. Singh (Associate) 2.90 Prepared deposition outline for plaintiff's CEO David Park; identified key documents for examination; drafted deposition notices for three additional witnesses.

03/07/24 M. Chen (Partner) 1.20 Reviewed and revised protective order motion; conferred with opposing counsel James Butler of Quinn Emanuel regarding stipulated protective order; negotiated confidentiality designations.

03/08/24 A. Singh (Associate) 6.10 Attended and defended deposition of client's VP of Engineering, Dr. Rachel Okafor; prepared witness for examination; interposed objections to 14 questions on privilege grounds.

03/11/24 M. Chen (Partner) 3.50 Reviewed expert report of Dr. Thomas Brennan regarding damages calculation; analyzed methodology for lost profits computation; conferred with damages expert regarding rebuttal opinions.

03/12/24 P. Lawson (Paralegal) 4.30 Filed motion for protective order with the court; served copies on all parties; calendared response deadline for March 26, 2024; updated case docket tracking spreadsheet.

03/13/24 A. Singh (Associate) 3.40 Drafted opposition to plaintiff's motion to compel production of source code; researched proportionality requirements under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26(b)(1); analyzed Waymo LLC v. Uber Technologies, Inc., No. 17-cv-00939 (N.D. Cal. 2017).

03/14/24 M. Chen (Partner) 2.80 Conferred with co-counsel regarding mediation preparation; drafted mediation brief outlining liability defenses and damages analysis; reviewed mediator's pre-session questionnaire.
03/15/24 A. Singh (Associate) 1.60 Finalized privilege log; reviewed and revised responses to interrogatories; prepared materials for client review and signature.

SUMMARY
Total Partner Hours: 12.20
Total Associate Hours: 21.90
Total Paralegal Hours: 10.10
Total All Timekeepers: 44.20
Total Fees (blended): $36,850.00
Total Costs Advanced: $2,145.00 (filing fees, deposition transcripts, Relativity hosting)

A Firm Non-Equity Partner is a member of a law firm who is not a shareholder but has a partnership-like role in the firm.
A Firm Partner/Shareholder is a lawyer who holds an ownership interest in a law firm, either as an equity or non-equity partner.

KIRKLAND & ELLIS LLP
BILLING NARRATIVE — CLIENT MATTER 4892-001
CLIENT: Westbrook Industries, Inc. v. Pacific Rim Supply Chain, LLC
PERIOD: March 1-15, 2024

DATE TIMEKEEPER HOURS DESCRIPTION
03/01/24 M. Chen (Partner) 2.40 Reviewed and analyzed plaintiff's first set of interrogatories; drafted preliminary responses to interrogatories 1-15; conferred with associate regarding document production timeline.

03/01/24 A. Singh (Associate) 3.70 Researched California law on trade secret misappropriation under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act; drafted memorandum analyzing elements of misappropriation claim; reviewed Silvaco Data Systems v. Intel Corp., 184 Cal. App. 4th 210 (2010).

03/02/24 M. Chen (Partner) 1.50 Conferred with client regarding settlement demand of $3,500,000.00; discussed litigation strategy and potential counterclaim for breach of the Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement dated January 5, 2023.

03/03/24 A. Singh (Associate) 4.20 Drafted motion for protective order regarding confidential business information; researched good cause standard under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26(c); analyzed In re Roman Catholic Archbishop of Portland, 661 F.3d 417 (9th Cir. 2011).

03/04/24 P. Lawson (Paralegal) 5.80 Organized and indexed 2,347 documents produced by plaintiff; prepared privilege log for 89 withheld documents; coded documents in Relativity for attorney review.

03/05/24 M. Chen (Partner) 0.80 Attended telephonic status conference with Judge Martinez; obtained extension of discovery deadline to April 30, 2024; reported conference results to client.

03/06/24 A. Singh (Associate) 2.90 Prepared deposition outline for plaintiff's CEO David Park; identified key documents for examination; drafted deposition notices for three additional witnesses.

03/07/24 M. Chen (Partner) 1.20 Reviewed and revised protective order motion; conferred with opposing counsel James Butler of Quinn Emanuel regarding stipulated protective order; negotiated confidentiality designations.

03/08/24 A. Singh (Associate) 6.10 Attended and defended deposition of client's VP of Engineering, Dr. Rachel Okafor; prepared witness for examination; interposed objections to 14 questions on privilege grounds.

03/11/24 M. Chen (Partner) 3.50 Reviewed expert report of Dr. Thomas Brennan regarding damages calculation; analyzed methodology for lost profits computation; conferred with damages expert regarding rebuttal opinions.

03/12/24 P. Lawson (Paralegal) 4.30 Filed motion for protective order with the court; served copies on all parties; calendared response deadline for March 26, 2024; updated case docket tracking spreadsheet.

03/13/24 A. Singh (Associate) 3.40 Drafted opposition to plaintiff's motion to compel production of source code; researched proportionality requirements under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 26(b)(1); analyzed Waymo LLC v. Uber Technologies, Inc., No. 17-cv-00939 (N.D. Cal. 2017).

03/14/24 M. Chen (Partner) 2.80 Conferred with co-counsel regarding mediation preparation; drafted mediation brief outlining liability defenses and damages analysis; reviewed mediator's pre-session questionnaire.

03/15/24 A. Singh (Associate) 1.60 Finalized privilege log; reviewed and revised responses to interrogatories; prepared materials for client review and signature.

SUMMARY
Total Partner Hours: 12.20
Total Associate Hours: 21.90
Total Paralegal Hours: 10.10
Total All Timekeepers: 44.20
Total Fees (blended): $36,850.00
Total Costs Advanced: $2,145.00 (filing fees, deposition transcripts, Relativity hosting)
